questions.question: What three bets did Whisper make against the wav2vec/HuBERT status quo?
options: Train on everything (680k hours of weakly-labeled internet audio across 97 languages, no phoneme labels), one multi-task model steered by task tokens (transcribe, translate, language ID, timestamps), and a plain encoder-decoder transformer — no vocoder, no CTC, no HMM
questions.explanation: The result is robustness across accents, noise, and zero-resource languages — the default speech front-end for nearly every voice assistant in 2026.

questions.question: In what sense is Whisper "a ViT that eats spectrograms"?
options: The log-mel spectrogram (~3,000 frames × 80 bins for 30s) is treated as the input image — a standard transformer encoder processes it, just as ViT processes patch sequences; audio became sequence modeling the same way vision did
It converts audio to actual photographs
It uses 16x16 pixel patches on waveforms
questions.explanation: Audio is an image of frequency over time. Same tokenize-then-transform recipe, different frontend.

questions.question: What does the convolutional stem accomplish?
options: Two Conv1D layers (kernel 3, stride 2) halve the 3,000 mel frames to 1,500 before the encoder — cutting sequence length (and quadratic attention cost) without many parameters
questions.explanation: A cheap learned downsampler at the entrance: the 24-layer encoder then runs self-attention over 1,500 timesteps instead of 3,000.

questions.question: How do task tokens steer the single decoder?
options: The decoder prompt begins with control tokens — <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> vs <|fr|><|translate|> — selecting language, task, and timestamping from one set of weights
questions.explanation: Multi-task-by-prompt predates the instruction-tuned LLM era's standard practice — the vocabulary is GPT-2's BPE plus these audio-specific specials.

questions.question: Why did weak supervision at scale beat clean-corpus pipelines?
options: 680k internet-scraped hours cover the real-world distribution — accents, noise, code-switching, domains — that curated academic corpora miss; volume and diversity bought robustness that no amount of pipeline polish on small clean data could
questions.explanation: The same scaling-era lesson as Chinchilla: data distribution coverage beats per-sample quality once the model is large enough to absorb the noise.
